skill.name: neon-postgres
skill.description: Expert patterns for Neon serverless Postgres, branching, connection pooling, and Prisma/Drizzle integration
skill.summary: Documentation skill for Neon Postgres database patterns including connection pooling, branching workflows, and ORM integration with Prisma and Drizzle.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 4 patterns that are all false positives. The skill contains documentation-only content with no executable code. Environment variable references are configuration examples, not actual env access. Cryptographic algorithm warnings are pattern-matching errors on unrelated text.

content.value_statement: Developers struggle with serverless database connection management and ORM configuration. This skill provides proven patterns for Neon Postgres with Prisma and Drizzle integration.
actual_capabilities: Configure Prisma Client with Neon pooled and direct connection strings
Set up Drizzle ORM with Neon serverless HTTP or WebSocket drivers
Implement connection pooling strategies using PgBouncer
Apply database branching workflows for development and testing
Optimize connection limits for serverless environments
limitations: Documentation only - does not execute database operations directly
Requires Neon Postgres account and database instance
Connection pooling limits depend on Neon plan tier

use_cases.title: Full-Stack Developer Setting Up New Project
use_cases.description: Configure database connections for a new Next.js project using Prisma with Neon Postgres, including proper separation of pooled and direct connections.

use_cases.title: Edge Application Database Integration
use_cases.description: Implement Drizzle ORM with Neon serverless driver for edge runtime compatibility in serverless functions.

use_cases.title: Database Migration Workflow
use_cases.description: Set up safe migration workflows using direct connections while maintaining pooled connections for application runtime.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Neon Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me configure a Neon Postgres connection string for my application. I need to understand the difference between pooled and direct connections.

prompt_templates.title: Prisma Schema Configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a Prisma schema configuration for Neon Postgres with proper datasource setup for both DATABASE_URL and DIRECT_URL environment variables.

prompt_templates.title: Drizzle Serverless Driver Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to configure Drizzle ORM with the Neon serverless driver for use in Vercel Edge Functions, including transaction support.

prompt_templates.title: Connection Pooling Optimization
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze my application connection patterns and recommend optimal PgBouncer pooling settings for Neon Postgres given my expected concurrent user load.

output_examples.output: Prisma datasource configured with dual connection strings: DATABASE_URL for pooled runtime connections (PgBouncer) and DIRECT_URL for direct migration operations. Schema includes proper provider and url settings.

output_examples.input: Set up Drizzle with Neon serverless driver
output_examples.output: Drizzle instance initialized with neon-serverless driver, enabling WebSocket-based sessions for transactions. HTTP driver available for simple queries in edge environments.

best_practices: Always use pooled connections for application runtime and direct connections for migrations
Reserve 7 connections for Neon superuser operations when calculating pool limits
Use database branching for isolated development and testing environments
anti_patterns: Using direct connection strings for application runtime connections
Exceeding connection pool limits without proper error handling
Running migrations against pooled connections instead of direct connections

faq.question: What is the difference between pooled and direct connections in Neon?
faq.answer: Pooled connections use PgBouncer for efficient connection reuse (up to 10K connections). Direct connections bypass the pooler and are required for DDL operations like migrations.

faq.question: How many connections can Neon handle?
faq.answer: Neon supports up to 10,000 concurrent connections to the pooler, with 7 connections reserved for the Neon superuser. Actual Postgres connections depend on your compute size.

faq.question: Can I use Neon with serverless edge functions?
faq.answer: Yes. Use Drizzle ORM with the neon-http driver for HTTP-based queries or neon-serverless driver for WebSocket transactions in edge environments.

faq.question: What is database branching in Neon?
faq.answer: Database branching creates instant copies of your database for isolated development, testing, or preview environments without duplicating storage.

faq.question: Do I need both Prisma and Drizzle?
faq.answer: No. Choose one ORM based on your needs. Prisma offers a full-featured ORM with migrations. Drizzle provides lighter weight with better serverless support.

faq.question: How do I handle connection errors in serverless environments?
faq.answer: Implement retry logic with exponential backoff. Use connection pooling to reduce connection overhead. Consider the neon-http driver for stateless query patterns.
